message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first ApprovalStore in allow-once gating (tick 274)

Migrates 2 ambient `ApprovalStore` call sites in the inline allow-once
approval gating path of `apply_approval_gate` (main.rs):

- L7355 approval consume path: `store.consume_with_context_with_cx(...)`
  — consumes an existing `--approval-code` token and returns an allow
  decision if the code matches, else re-requires approval.
- L7374 approval issue path: `store.issue_with_cx(...)` — issues a new
  approval token when the decision requires approval but no code was
  provided; the caller renders the code via `build_inline_approval_retry_command`.

Both sites resolve cx via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`.
The `_with_cx` variants thread cx through the internal storage-layer
calls for token insert, lookup, audit write, and use-count increment.

This closes the remaining ambient approval-store surface in main.rs:
`apply_approval_gate` is called from every policy-gated CLI dispatch
(event annotate, plan execute, pane send, etc), so cx-awareness here
propagates broadly — a cx-cancel during approval-gated work now cleanly
aborts the gate check rather than writing a partial audit record before
unwinding.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 88 (187-274).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 150 sites (148 before + 2).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 45 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
